situation: Received feedback on my project with some constructive criticism
automatic_thought: I completely failed. My work is totally useless.
evidence_for: There were 3 areas marked for improvement
The feedback wasn't 100% positive
evidence_against: The reviewer said the overall concept was strong
5 sections were marked as excellent
My manager praised my creativity
balanced_thought: My project had both strengths and areas for improvement. The criticism doesn't erase the good work I did.

situation: Got a B+ on my final exam instead of an A
automatic_thought: If I don't get an A, I've completely failed. I'm not smart enough.
evidence_for: I aimed for an A and didn't achieve it
I made a few mistakes on the exam
evidence_against: B+ is still a good grade
One grade doesn't define my intelligence
I've gotten As on other exams
balanced_thought: A B+ demonstrates good understanding. I can learn from my mistakes without labeling myself a failure.

situation: Burned dinner while cooking for guests
automatic_thought: I ruined everything. I'm a terrible cook and host.
evidence_for: The main dish was burned
I felt embarrassed serving takeout
evidence_against: The appetizers and dessert were perfect
Guests said they enjoyed the evening
I've cooked successful meals before
balanced_thought: One burned dish doesn't ruin an entire evening. My guests cared more about the company than the food.

situation: Didn't get the promotion I applied for
automatic_thought: I'm either the best or I'm nothing. I'm obviously not good enough.
evidence_for: I didn't get the promotion
Someone else was chosen over me
evidence_against: My manager said I was a strong candidate
I received positive feedback
I can apply again in the future
balanced_thought: Not getting this promotion is disappointing, but it doesn't make me worthless. I have value regardless of my job title.

situation: Made a mistake during my presentation at work
automatic_thought: I messed up one slide, so the whole presentation was a disaster.
evidence_for: I stumbled over one data point
I felt unprofessional in that moment
evidence_against: The other 19 slides went smoothly
Colleagues complimented me afterward
Everyone makes minor errors
balanced_thought: One error in a 20-slide presentation doesn't make it a disaster. The overall message was delivered effectively.

situation: Got rejected from a job I really wanted
automatic_thought: I'll never get hired anywhere. I'm unemployable.
evidence_for: This was my third rejection this month
The job market feels competitive
evidence_against: I've been employed before and succeeded
Rejection is normal in job searching
I have skills employers value
balanced_thought: This rejection is disappointing but doesn't predict my entire future. I can learn from this and keep trying.

situation: My date didn't want a second date
automatic_thought: No one will ever want to date me. I'm destined to be alone.
evidence_for: This person wasn't interested
I've been single for a while
evidence_against: I've had relationships before
Not every date will be a match
I have qualities people find attractive
balanced_thought: This one date not working out doesn't mean I'll never find someone. Compatibility is complex and takes time.

situation: Stumbled over my words during a toast at a wedding
automatic_thought: I'm always awkward in public speaking. I make a fool of myself every time.
evidence_for: I felt nervous and stumbled a bit
It wasn't as smooth as I hoped
evidence_against: I've given successful speeches before
People smiled and applauded afterward
Being nervous doesn't equal being terrible
balanced_thought: I stumbled a bit, but I've spoken successfully before. One moment doesn't define my public speaking ability.

situation: My friend was short with me on the phone
automatic_thought: People are always mean to me. No one really cares about me.
evidence_for: My friend seemed distracted
I felt dismissed in that moment
evidence_against: My friend has been supportive many times
They might have been busy or stressed
People have off days
balanced_thought: My friend might have been having a bad day. One brief call doesn't mean no one cares about me.

situation: Burned the cookies I was baking
automatic_thought: I can't cook anything right. I'm useless in the kitchen.
evidence_for: The cookies were burned
This is the second baking mistake this month
evidence_against: I've successfully cooked many meals before
Baking requires precision and everyone makes mistakes
balanced_thought: I made a mistake with the cookies, but I've cooked successfully before. I'm learning and improving.

situation: My boss didn't say good morning to me
automatic_thought: She's angry with me about something. She must think I'm doing a terrible job.
evidence_for: She walked past without making eye contact
She seemed serious this morning
evidence_against: She was on her phone and might not have noticed me
She praised my work in yesterday's meeting
She smiled at me later
balanced_thought: I don't know why my boss didn't say good morning. There could be many explanations unrelated to me or my work.

situation: My friends went to lunch without inviting me
automatic_thought: They're excluding me on purpose. They must be talking about me behind my back.
evidence_for: I saw them together at the restaurant
I wasn't invited
evidence_against: They might have planned it spontaneously
They've included me in many other activities
They texted me later asking about my day
balanced_thought: I don't know why I wasn't invited. There are many innocent explanations, and assuming the worst isn't helpful.

situation: A stranger looked at me and then looked away quickly
automatic_thought: They think I look weird. They're judging my appearance negatively.
evidence_for: They looked at me and then away
I felt self-conscious in that moment
evidence_against: I don't know what they were thinking
People look around constantly in public
Strangers' opinions don't define me
balanced_thought: I can't know what a stranger is thinking. Their glance likely had nothing to do with me.

situation: My partner has been quiet this evening
automatic_thought: They're losing interest in me. They must be thinking about ending the relationship.
evidence_for: They haven't talked much tonight
They seem distant
evidence_against: They had a stressful day at work
They said I love you this morning
Everyone needs quiet time sometimes
balanced_thought: My partner is probably just tired or preoccupied. I should ask how they're feeling rather than assuming the worst.

situation: My colleague didn't respond to my email immediately
automatic_thought: She thinks my ideas are stupid and not worth responding to.
evidence_for: It's been 3 hours and no response
The email was important to me
evidence_against: She might be in meetings
She often takes time to respond
Response time doesn't indicate opinion quality
balanced_thought: There are many reasons for a delayed email response. Assuming negative judgment isn't based on facts.

situation: Have a headache that won't go away
automatic_thought: This is definitely a brain tumor. I'm going to die and leave my family alone.
evidence_for: The headache has lasted all day
It's more painful than usual
evidence_against: I've had headaches before that went away
Most headaches are benign
I can see a doctor if concerned
balanced_thought: While this headache is uncomfortable, it's most likely stress-related. I can monitor it and see a doctor if it persists.

situation: Running late for an important meeting
automatic_thought: I'm going to get fired. This is a disaster. My career is over.
evidence_for: Being late is unprofessional
This is an important meeting
evidence_against: Everyone is occasionally late
Being late once doesn't result in firing
I have a good track record
balanced_thought: Being late is unfortunate but not career-ending. I can apologize and make sure it doesn't become a pattern.

situation: Economy is showing signs of recession
automatic_thought: I'm definitely going to lose my job and become homeless. I'll never recover financially.
evidence_for: The economy is uncertain
Some companies are laying off workers
evidence_against: Not all recessions affect all industries equally
I have emergency savings
I have marketable skills
balanced_thought: While economic uncertainty is real, catastrophizing doesn't help. I can focus on what I can control.

situation: Feeling anxious about an upcoming flight
automatic_thought: The plane is going to crash. I'm going to die. I shouldn't get on that plane.
evidence_for: Plane crashes do happen occasionally
I feel very anxious about flying
evidence_against: Flying is statistically the safest mode of travel
Millions of flights land safely every day
My anxiety is a feeling, not a prediction
balanced_thought: My anxiety makes me feel like something bad will happen, but statistics show flying is very safe. My feelings aren't facts.

situation: My child has a fever and cough
automatic_thought: This is going to turn into something serious. My child might die and it will be all my fault.
evidence_for: My child is sick and uncomfortable
Illnesses can sometimes become serious
evidence_against: Children get sick frequently and usually recover quickly
Most childhood illnesses are mild
I have access to medical care
balanced_thought: My child has a common illness. While it's worrying, most childhood illnesses resolve with proper care and monitoring.

situation: Feeling overwhelmed with work and family responsibilities
automatic_thought: I should be able to handle all of this without getting stressed. What's wrong with me?
evidence_for: Other people seem to manage similar responsibilities
I expect myself to be capable
evidence_against: Everyone has limits and gets stressed sometimes
Stress is a normal human response
Perfection is impossible
balanced_thought: I'm human, and stress is a normal response to heavy demands. I can be kind to myself while managing my responsibilities.

situation: Made a decision that didn't work out as planned
automatic_thought: I should have known better. I'm so stupid for not seeing this coming.
evidence_for: The outcome was negative
In hindsight, I can see warning signs
evidence_against: I made the best decision I could with the information I had
Hindsight is always clearer
Everyone makes decisions that don't work out
balanced_thought: I made the best choice I could with what I knew then. I can learn from this without harsh self-judgment.

situation: Comparing my career progress to peers
automatic_thought: I should be further along in my career by now. I'm falling behind everyone else.
evidence_for: Some peers have higher titles or salaries
I had expected to achieve more by this age
evidence_against: Everyone's timeline is different
Life isn't a race with a single finish line
I'm exactly where I need to be for my journey
balanced_thought: There's no 'right' timeline for success. I'm on my own path with my own unique circumstances and achievements.

situation: My partner didn't anticipate my needs
automatic_thought: They should know what I need without me having to tell them. If they loved me, they'd just know.
evidence_for: I feel like my partner should understand me
It would feel more romantic if they just knew
evidence_against: No one is a mind reader
Clear communication leads to better relationships
Different people show love differently
balanced_thought: People aren't mind readers. Clear communication helps my partner understand me better and strengthens our relationship.

situation: Made a mistake at work
automatic_thought: I should never make mistakes. Perfection is the minimum standard.
evidence_for: Mistakes can have consequences
I hold myself to high standards
evidence_against: Everyone makes mistakes - it's part of being human
Mistakes are opportunities to learn
Perfection is an impossible standard
balanced_thought: Mistakes are part of learning and growth. I can strive for excellence without demanding impossible perfection.

situation: Feeling down and unmotivated today
automatic_thought: I feel like a failure, so I must be a failure. My feelings prove it's true.
evidence_for: I feel unsuccessful and defeated
The feeling is very strong and persistent
evidence_against: Feelings are not facts - they're temporary states
I've had successes in the past
My accomplishments still exist
balanced_thought: I feel like a failure right now, but that feeling isn't proof of fact. I'm experiencing a difficult emotion, not an objective truth.

situation: Feeling anxious about accepting a new opportunity
automatic_thought: I feel anxious about this decision, so it must be the wrong choice. My anxiety is warning me.
evidence_for: My gut feeling says this is risky
I feel very nervous about it
evidence_against: Anxiety often accompanies important decisions, even good ones
Growth happens outside my comfort zone
Fear of change is different from actual danger
balanced_thought: Anxiety often accompanies important changes. I should evaluate the decision based on facts, not just my emotional response.

situation: Looking in the mirror and feeling unattractive
automatic_thought: I feel ugly today, so I must actually look terrible. My feelings reflect reality.
evidence_for: I feel very unattractive right now
The feeling seems very real and true
evidence_against: My appearance hasn't changed overnight
Self-esteem fluctuates based on mood
This feeling will pass
balanced_thought: My appearance hasn't changed; my mood has. This feeling will pass, and it doesn't reflect objective reality.

situation: Feeling guilty after setting a boundary with a friend
automatic_thought: I feel guilty, so I must have done something wrong. Guilt means I'm bad.
evidence_for: The guilt feels very intense
I feel bad about saying no
evidence_against: Setting healthy boundaries is necessary, not wrong
Guilt can be triggered by unrealistic expectations
I have a right to prioritize my own needs
balanced_thought: Setting boundaries is healthy, even if it feels uncomfortable. My guilt reflects my caring nature, not wrongdoing.

situation: Feeling overwhelmed by a complex task
automatic_thought: I feel overwhelmed, so this task must be impossible. I can't do it.
evidence_for: The task feels impossible right now
I feel paralyzed by the complexity
evidence_against: I've completed complex tasks before
Breaking tasks down makes them manageable
Feeling overwhelmed is a signal to strategize
balanced_thought: Feeling overwhelmed signals I need to break this down, not that it's impossible. I can tackle this step by step.

situation: Received 10 compliments and 1 criticism at work
automatic_thought: That one criticism proves I'm doing everything wrong. The compliments don't count.
evidence_for: The criticism was specific and pointed out real areas for improvement
evidence_against: I received 10 compliments from colleagues and clients
The criticism was minor compared to the positive feedback
Most people focus on my strengths
balanced_thought: I received mostly positive feedback with one area to improve. The criticism is valuable but doesn't negate the compliments.

situation: One person at the party seemed bored talking to me
automatic_thought: I must be boring. The whole party was a failure because of that one interaction.
evidence_for: One person seemed disinterested
They checked their phone during our conversation
evidence_against: I had engaging conversations with 5 other people
Several people laughed at my jokes
The host thanked me for coming
balanced_thought: Most people seemed to enjoy talking to me. One person's mood doesn't define my social skills.

situation: Performance review was mostly positive but mentioned one growth area
automatic_thought: The criticism means I'm failing at my job. The positive parts don't matter.
evidence_for: My manager mentioned I could be more proactive
This is an area I need to work on
evidence_against: The review was rated 'exceeds expectations' overall
My manager praised my technical skills
I received a raise based on the review
balanced_thought: The review was largely positive with constructive feedback for growth. This is normal and healthy.

situation: Made a small typo in an email after a productive day
automatic_thought: I'm so careless. This typo ruins my entire day of good work.
evidence_for: I made a spelling error in an important email
It was noticed by the recipient
evidence_against: I completed 15 other tasks perfectly today
The typo didn't change the meaning of the message
Everyone makes minor errors
balanced_thought: One typo doesn't erase a day of productive work. I can correct it and move on.

situation: Vacation had 5 sunny days and 1 rainy afternoon
automatic_thought: The rain ruined the whole trip. It was a waste of money.
evidence_for: We missed the beach that afternoon
The rain was disappointing
evidence_against: We had 5 beautiful days of sunshine
We explored indoor museums during the rain
The overall experience was wonderful
balanced_thought: One rainy afternoon doesn't ruin a week-long vacation. We adapted and still had a great time.

situation: Got promoted at work
automatic_thought: I only got promoted because they were short on managers, not because I'm actually qualified.
evidence_for: The company did need to fill the position quickly
The hiring process was expedited
evidence_against: I have 5 years of relevant experience
I've exceeded targets consistently
My manager specifically recommended me
balanced_thought: While timing played a role, I was promoted because of my skills and performance. I earned this.

situation: Friend complimented my kindness
automatic_thought: She's just saying that to be nice. She doesn't really mean it.
evidence_for: People sometimes give compliments to be polite
I find it hard to accept praise
evidence_against: My friend is usually honest and direct
She gave specific examples of my kindness
Other people have said similar things
balanced_thought: My friend gave me a genuine compliment. I can accept that I have positive qualities others notice.

situation: Finished a project on time
automatic_thought: Anyone could have done that. It's not a real achievement.
evidence_for: The project wasn't extremely complex
Other people have done similar work
evidence_against: I managed the timeline effectively
I solved several unexpected problems
My client was satisfied with the result
balanced_thought: Completing a project on time requires planning, effort, and skill. This is a legitimate accomplishment.

situation: Received a scholarship for my studies
automatic_thought: It was probably because not many people applied. I'm not actually smart.
evidence_for: The application pool might have been smaller this year
I got lucky with the essay topic
evidence_against: Scholarships are competitive regardless of applicant numbers
I have strong academic performance
The committee specifically praised my essay
balanced_thought: I earned this scholarship through my academic performance and potential. External circumstances don't negate my merit.

situation: Partner expressed love and appreciation
automatic_thought: They only love me because they haven't realized how flawed I am yet.
evidence_for: I do have flaws and imperfections
I'm afraid of being truly seen
evidence_against: My partner has seen me at my worst and stayed
They've known me for years
Love includes accepting imperfections
balanced_thought: My partner sees my flaws and chooses to love me anyway. Their love is based on knowing the real me.

situation: Made a mistake on a work report
automatic_thought: I'm a complete idiot and a total failure. This proves I'm incompetent.
evidence_for: I made an error in the report
The mistake was embarrassing
evidence_against: I've successfully completed dozens of reports before
Mistakes are part of learning
My overall performance is strong
balanced_thought: I made a mistake, but that doesn't define my entire identity. I'm a capable person who had a moment of error.

situation: Another driver cut me off in traffic
automatic_thought: What a jerk! People are so selfish and terrible.
evidence_for: The driver acted inconsiderately
They didn't signal before changing lanes
evidence_against: I don't know their situation - maybe they were rushing to an emergency
Most drivers are careful and polite
One action doesn't define a person's character
balanced_thought: That driver made an inconsiderate move, but I don't know their situation. One action doesn't make them a terrible person.

situation: Didn't get the job I interviewed for
automatic_thought: I'm unemployable and worthless. No one will ever hire me.
evidence_for: I didn't get this specific job
The rejection stings
evidence_against: I've been employed before and succeeded
I have valuable skills and experience
The right opportunity will come along
balanced_thought: I didn't get this specific job, but I'm skilled and valuable. This rejection doesn't define my worth.

situation: Friend canceled our plans last minute
automatic_thought: She's so flaky and unreliable. I can't count on anyone.
evidence_for: She canceled at the last minute
This has happened before
evidence_against: She's been there for me many times before
She might have a good reason for canceling
I shouldn't label her entire character based on one incident
balanced_thought: My friend canceled once, but she's been reliable in the past. I shouldn't label her entire character based on one incident.

situation: Forgot to call my mom back
automatic_thought: I'm such a terrible daughter and a bad person.
evidence_for: I forgot to return the call
My mom was waiting to hear from me
evidence_against: I call my mom regularly and we have a good relationship
One instance of forgetfulness doesn't make me terrible
I can apologize and make it right
balanced_thought: I forgot to call back, but that doesn't make me a terrible person. I can call now and apologize.

situation: My child is struggling in school
automatic_thought: It's all my fault for being a bad parent. I should have done more.
evidence_for: I could have helped more with homework
I work long hours
evidence_against: My child's struggles have many factors - learning style, school environment, social dynamics
I support my child in many ways
Blaming myself entirely isn't helpful
balanced_thought: My child's struggles have multiple causes. I can support them without taking all the blame.

situation: My friend seems depressed lately
automatic_thought: I must have done something to upset them. It's my fault they're sad.
evidence_for: I haven't been as available lately
I feel responsible for my friends' wellbeing
evidence_against: Depression has many causes - biological, situational, chemical
My friend has other support systems
Assuming I'm the cause is self-centered
balanced_thought: My friend's depression likely has many causes. I can offer support without assuming I'm the cause.

situation: My team lost the game
automatic_thought: If I had played better, we would have won. It's all on me.
evidence_for: I missed a shot that could have tied the game
I feel responsible for the team's performance
evidence_against: Team sports involve many players and factors
My teammates also made mistakes
The other team played well too
balanced_thought: I contributed to the game, but I don't control the entire outcome. Teams win and lose together.

situation: My partner is in a bad mood
automatic_thought: I must have done something wrong to cause it. What did I do?
evidence_for: We had a small disagreement yesterday
I feel responsible for their happiness
evidence_against: My partner mentioned work stress this morning
They said it's not about me
Everyone has bad days unrelated to their partner
balanced_thought: My partner's mood could be due to many factors. I shouldn't automatically assume it's about me.

situation: The dinner party I hosted felt awkward
automatic_thought: I must be terrible at hosting. I ruined everything.
evidence_for: There were some awkward silences
Not everyone seemed to click
evidence_against: Group dynamics are complex and not solely my responsibility
Some guests said they had a good time
Awkward moments happen at every gathering
balanced_thought: I facilitated the gathering, but I can't control how every interaction goes. Group dynamics are complex.

situation: Failed an exam
automatic_thought: I failed because my teacher is terrible and doesn't know how to teach. It's not my fault at all.
evidence_for: The lectures were confusing
The exam questions seemed unfair
evidence_against: Other students passed the same exam
I could have studied differently
I have control over my preparation
balanced_thought: External factors played a role, but I also could have prepared differently. I should focus on what I can control for next time.

situation: Struggling with weight management
automatic_thought: I'm overweight because fast food companies manipulate people. It's not my fault at all.
evidence_for: Marketing does influence food choices
Unhealthy food is heavily advertised
evidence_against: I make my own food choices
I have access to healthy food options
I can learn about nutrition and make changes
balanced_thought: While marketing plays a role, I make my own food choices. I can take responsibility for my health decisions.

situation: Marriage ended in divorce
automatic_thought: My marriage failed because my ex is a narcissist. I had nothing to do with it.
evidence_for: My ex had significant issues
They were unwilling to change
evidence_against: Relationships involve two people
I can reflect on my own patterns
Blaming entirely prevents growth
balanced_thought: While my ex had issues, I can also reflect on my own patterns to grow for future relationships.

situation: Not advancing at work as quickly as desired
automatic_thought: I can't get ahead because the system is rigged against people like me. There's no point trying.
evidence_for: There are systemic barriers in my industry
I've faced discrimination
evidence_against: Many people from similar backgrounds do succeed
I can still develop skills and seek opportunities
Giving up guarantees failure
balanced_thought: While systemic barriers exist, I can still focus on developing skills and seeking opportunities within my control.

situation: Financial difficulties
automatic_thought: I'm broke because the economy is terrible and my parents didn't teach me about money. It's never my fault.
evidence_for: The economy has been challenging
I didn't receive financial education growing up
evidence_against: I can learn financial skills now
I make spending decisions daily
Many people overcome similar backgrounds
balanced_thought: External factors affect finances, but I can also learn budgeting and financial skills now to improve my situation.

situation: Thinking about applying for a dream job
automatic_thought: I'll never get this job, so there's no point in applying. I know I'll be rejected.
evidence_for: The job is competitive
I don't meet every single requirement
evidence_against: I won't know unless I try
I have transferable skills
Not trying guarantees failure
balanced_thought: I don't know what will happen. I have a chance if I apply, and not trying guarantees failure.

situation: Considering asking someone on a date
automatic_thought: They'll definitely say no. I can tell they're not interested in me.
evidence_for: I haven't noticed obvious signs of interest
They might not be looking for a relationship
evidence_against: I can't read their mind
They might be interested but shy too
Rejection isn't the end of the world
balanced_thought: I don't know how they'll respond unless I ask. Fear of rejection shouldn't stop me from trying.

situation: Planning to start a new business
automatic_thought: My business will definitely fail. Most new businesses fail within the first year.
evidence_for: Statistics show many businesses fail
The market is competitive
evidence_against: Many businesses also succeed
I can learn from failures
Not starting guarantees no success
balanced_thought: While risks exist, I can't predict the future. I can plan carefully and adapt as I learn.

situation: About to take an important test
automatic_thought: I'm going to fail this test. I just know I won't remember anything.
evidence_for: I feel very anxious
I struggled with some material
evidence_against: I've passed tests before
I've studied and prepared
Anxiety doesn't predict performance
balanced_thought: I've prepared and passed tests before. My anxiety doesn't predict my actual performance.

situation: Haven't heard back from a job application
automatic_thought: They've definitely rejected me and just haven't sent the email yet. I should give up.
evidence_for: It's been two weeks with no response
They might have found another candidate
evidence_against: Hiring processes often take time
No response doesn't mean rejection
I should follow up before assuming
balanced_thought: I don't know the status yet. I should follow up rather than assuming the worst.
